8079.note: Reproduced. Self-contained minimal repro: a class with both a method named after a site builtin (license/copyright/credits/exit/quit) AND that name also bound as an instance attribute via self.NAME = ... in __init__. This path enters class_checker.visit_functiondef's ancestor.lookup(node.name) branch which raises StatementMissing on Module.builtins. Confirmed crash on pylint 3.3.8/astroid 3.3.11, pylint 4.0.5/astroid 4.0.2 (THIS REPO), pylint 4.0.5/astroid 4.0.4, AND pylint 4.1.0-dev0/astroid 4.2.0b3 (latest main). Previous FIXED determination was incorrect — it was based on the simpler sub-case 'tuple = namedtuple(None, [])' which IS fixed in astroid 4.0.0, but that's a different code path (safe_infer → pytype → ancestors) than the funkwhale-flavored one (class_checker → ancestor.lookup → _filter_stmts → statement). The deeper bug is still live.
